verdict_reasoning: EREG (epiregulin) is synthesized as a single-pass ~162-aa transmembrane glycoprotein precursor (~19 kDa) with the mature EGF domain in its extracellular region. ADAM sheddases cleave the precursor to release the ~6 kDa/46-aa soluble mature ligand that acts on EGFR and ErbB4. The TM precursor stage is a real, bona fide surface form (documented at the basolateral cell surface of epithelial cells), enabling juxtacrine signaling. However, the dominant biological actor is the shed soluble form; the membrane-anchored stage is transient. This fits the 'transient TM precursor of a shed-ligand-dominant gene' pattern: the surface state is real but short-lived, making contextual / dual_localization the correct call.
